description: Shared shape for incident-ledger rows the iOS client PRODUCES (IncidentLedgerRecord) and the Cloudflare worker VALIDATES on bug-report submission (sanitizeIncidentLedgerEntry). The two are hand-mirrored; a tightening on one side that is not mirrored on the other silently strips rows in prod — the exact observability OBS-R2 exists to guarantee. Both test suites LOAD this file, so a divergence fails CI. Reason cases are un-padded kebab labels — exactly what the producer emits; surrounding-whitespace handling is deliberately out of scope (the worker trims via getOptionalString, the Swift writer does not, but the producer never pads).
